members.name: scalingMonitorIndex
members.description: The index of the monitor whose DPI scale is being used to for the Window. This is the usually the monitor the window is covering the most.

members.name: isBorders
members.description: True if the window is bordered.

members.name: isFill
members.description: True if the window will stretch its contents to fill its specified area.

members.name: width
members.description: Window width. Expressed in points or pixels, depending on the DPI Scaling parameter of the Window COMP.

members.name: height
members.description: Window height. Expressed in points or pixels, depending on the DPI Scaling parameter of the Window COMP.

members.name: x
members.description: Window X coordinate relative to the bottom left of the main monitor. Expressed in points or pixels, depending on the DPI Scaling parameter of the Window COMP.

members.name: y
members.description: Window Y coordinate relative to the bottom left of the main monitor. Expressed in points or pixels, depending on the DPI Scaling parameter of the Window COMP.

members.name: contentX
members.description: X position of left edge of the windows contents. Ignores borders if they are present. Expressed in points or pixels, depending on the 'DPI Scaling' parameter setting.

members.name: contentY
members.description: Y position of bottom edge of the windows contents. Ignores borders if they are present. Expressed in points or pixels, depending on the 'DPI Scaling' parameter setting.

members.name: contentWidth
members.description: Width of windows contents. Ignores borders if they are present. Expressed in points or pixels, depending on the 'DPI Scaling' parameter setting.

members.name: contentHeight
members.description: Height of windows contents. Ignores borders if they are present. Expressed in points or pixels, depending on the 'DPI Scaling' parameter setting.

methods.name: setForeground
methods.description: Activates the window, sets it to the foregound and other visual cues. Sets focus and increases process priority. Can only be called by a foreground process, or a child of a foreground process. Returns true if successful.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the WindowCOMP Class wiki page
